evals.prompt: I have this Python library called fastcache that I wrote for work. It's at ~/projects/fastcache/. Can you create a skill for it so Claude can help people use it?
evals.expected_output: A complete skill directory with SKILL.md, scripts, and references targeting the fastcache library
expectations: Generated skill has valid SKILL.md with frontmatter
Analysis correctly identifies target as a library
Skill includes usage examples from the library

evals.prompt: Make a multi-platform skill for the httpie CLI tool. I want it to work on Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Codex.
evals.expected_output: Skill packaged for all three platforms in dist/ directory
expectations: Analysis detects httpie as a CLI tool
dist/ contains claude-code, openclaw, and codex directories
Each platform has a valid SKILL.md with correct frontmatter

evals.prompt: I want to turn this workflow into a skill: first pull data from our Postgres DB, then clean it with pandas, then upload to S3. Create a skill that guides Claude through this.
expectations: Dependencies include postgres, pandas, and boto3

evals.prompt: Generate a skill for the Stripe API. Focus on payment intents and customer management.
evals.expected_output: API-type skill with endpoint references and auth patterns
expectations: Analysis identifies Stripe as an API target

evals.prompt: Create a skill for jq and optimize the description so it triggers accurately. Run the full pipeline including benchmarks.
evals.expected_output: Complete skill with optimized description and benchmark results
expectations: Description optimization ran with train/test split
